text:
In consequence of the fact that this court has ordered that the child[ren] shall remain [a] ward[s] of this court whilst they remain[s] [a] minor, this court is empowered and required to exercise its custodial jurisdiction over them and to ascertain their best interests and to facilitate and promote those best interests.

It appears that the child[ren] [was] / [were] habitually resident in England and Wales at the time that they were removed from this jurisdiction on the [date of removal] [and should not have been retained outside the jurisdiction beyond [date of retention]].

This court is satisfied that all interested parties are before the court [including Cafcass, which has been appointed by the court to represent the child[ren]].

This court has, considering it to be in the child[ren]’s best interests, determined that they should be returned as soon as practicable to England and Wales.

It appears to this court on a provisional basis on the basis of the evidence filed that:

The child[ren] [was] / [were] on [date of removal or retention] and remained on [date of commencement of proceedings] habitually resident in the jurisdiction of England and Wales,

The child[ren] [was] / [were] wrongfully [removed from England] / [retained outside the jurisdiction of England] on [date],

The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction in matters of parental responsibility over the child[ren] pursuant to [Articles 8 and 10 of BIIR] / [Articles 5 and 7 of the 1996 Hague Convention].

(the court may consider making these declarations if the respondent(s) have been served or genuine attempts to serve are proved)

Undertakings to the court by the applicant (delete or supplement as appropriate)

The applicant undertakes:

Not to remove the child[ren] from the care of the respondent until further order of this court.

[insert]

Undertakings to the court by the solicitors for the applicant (if appropriate)

The solicitors for the applicant undertake to:

[insert]

Mediation
